Merrill College Data Journalism Training

Dow Jones News Fund @ Merrill College

Sunday, May 31 - Sunday, June 7, 2026

(ver March 26, 2026)


Rob Wells, director
Karen Denny, co-director  
Bridget Lang, assistant

Itinerary

Pre-Conference

  • By Monday May 25: Students will have data assignments described.
  • By May 25: Students will be sent a data starter kit to load relevant software on their laptops and test out their equipment.
  • By May 28: Students profiles due, 11:59 p.m.

Sunday, May 31, 2026

  • 2 p.m.: Welcome at Knight Hall Eaton Theater. Wells Denny, Lang and Shirley Carswell, DJNF Executive Director
    • Logistics. The week ahead. Rooms meals.
    • Student introductions.
  • 3:30 p.m.: Use data to find stories.
    • New to Coding Experienced Track: . Eaton Theater
    • Coding Experienced Track: Wells. Room 1109
  • 4:30 p.m.: Break and then independent work
    • Students review data memos for their assigned communities. Produce draft story ideas by 11:59 p.m.
  • 5:30 p.m.: Break
  • 6:00 p.m.: Dinner in Hyattsville - (Meet at Knight Hall board van)
  • 11:59 p.m.: Produce one paragraph draft story ideas by 11:59 p.m., emailed to Wells

Monday, June 1, 2026

  • Breakfast: On your own

  • 9 a.m.: Meet at Knight Hall

  • Skills sessions. Pick one

    • Coding Experienced Track: Data Driven Reporting. (Aidan Hughes, the Howard Center)
      • Bring material into R and make sense of it.
      • Basic webscraping, PDF data extraction
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • New to Coding Track: Importing Data. (Wells)
      • Basic spreadsheets to R.
      • Knight Hall Room 2107

  • 10:30 a.m.: Break. Coffee served. Lobby.

  • 11 a.m.: Workshops: Use data to find stories. (Wells, Hughes). Rooms 2103, 2107

  • 12:30 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall.

  • 1:30 p.m.: Writing a story pitch memo. All attend. (Denny). Room 2103

    • Slides for talk

  • 2:15 p.m.: Using GitHub. All attend. (Wells/Lang) GitHub refresher

  • 2:45 p.m.: Workshop for story pitch memo due at 11:59 p.m. (Wells, Denny, Lang coach). Rooms 2103, 2107

  • 4 p.m.: Diversity is Good Journalism: Covering All of the Community. (Prof. Christoph Mergerson). All attend. Room 2103

    • Readings before talk

  • 5:30 p.m.: Break

  • 6:30 p.m.: Dinner on campus

  • 11:59 p.m.: Draft story pitch memo, emailed to Wells

Tuesday June 2, 2026

  • Breakfast: On your own

  • 9 a.m. - 10:30 a.m.: Skills sessions

    • Coding Experienced Track: Using AI with R. (Derek Willis, the Howard Center)
      • Use R to link to a LLM and process data.
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • New to Coding Track: Data cleaning. (TK TK)
      • Use Open Refine, R to clean data
      • Knight Hall Room 2107

  • 10:30 a.m.: Break. Coffee served.

  • 11 a.m.: Workshop. Students work on data gathering story pitches. (Wells, Willis, TA as coaches). Room 1101

  • 1 p.m.-2:30 p.m.: Data Visualization. . Using Datawrapper for data visualization. (Adam Marton). All attend. Room 2103

  • 2:30 p.m.-2:45 p.m. : Break

  • 2:45 p.m.-4 p.m. : Workshop on your projects. (Wells / Marton / Lang)

  • 4 p.m.-5 p.m.: Speaker: Prof. Kevin Blackistone on racial justice in sports business

  • 5:30 p.m.: Break

  • 6:30 p.m.: Dinner in Hyattsville. (Meet at Knight Hall board van. Wells / driving)

Wednesday June 3, 2026

  • Breakfast: On your own

  • 9 a.m.-10:15 a.m.: Skills sessions

    • Coding Experienced Track: Using AI with R. (Daniel Trielli, Merrill College)
      • Use R to link to a LLM and process data.
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • New to Coding Experienced Track: Analyzing your data (Wells)
      • Using the Tidyverse to summarize and analyze data.
      • Knight Hall Room 2107

  • 10:15 a.m.: Break. Coffee served.

  • 10:30 a.m.: Workshop. Open workshop on projects. (Wells, Trielli, Lang coach). Rooms 2103, 2107

  • 11:30 a.m. - 12:30 p.m.: Keynote speaker: Jon Schleuss, President, The News Guild. All attend. Eaton Theater.

    • Readings before Jon Schleuss talk TK TK TK
    • Lunch with Schleuss

  • 1:30 p.m. - 2:30 p.m.: Introduction to business reporting. Wells proposals. Eaton Theater

  • 2:30 p.m. - 3 p.m.: Digital accessibility for data visualization. Lang. Eaton Theater

  • 3 p.m. - 6 p.m.: Workshop on your projects. Wells / Lang / Denny

  • 6 p.m.: Dinner on campus

  • 11:59 p.m.: Second draft of story pitch memos due, emailed to Wells and .

Thursday June 4, 2026

  • Breakfast: On your own at dorm
  • 8:15 a.m.: Meet at Knight Hall, board van, depart for College Park Metro (Wells driving)
  • 10 a.m.: Tour of AP D.C. bureau with Chad Day, AP’s elections team.
  • Noon: Lunch.
  • 1:30 p.m.: Tour U.S. Capitol press galleries TK TK TK with Stephen Neukam, Axios and Ed Kachinske, U.S. House Press Gallery.
  • 6 p.m.: Dinner in Washington D.C.

Friday June 5, 2026

  • Breakfast: On your own at dorm
  • 9 a.m.-10 a.m.: Skills to survive in the newsroom. Time management, deadlines. All attend. (Denny) Eaton Theater.
  • 10:00 a.m. - 10:15 a.m.: Break
  • 10:15 a.m.-12:30 p.m.: Workshop on projects (Wells, Denny, Lang coach)
  • 12:30 p.m.-1:30 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall
  • 3:30 p.m.: Meet at Knight Hall, board van, depart for College Park Metro, dinner in Washington D.C., attend a community jazz show at Westminster Church. Meet with Pastor Brian E. Hamilton beforehand to discuss Southwest D.C. history

Saturday June 6, 2026

  • Breakfast: On your own at dorm
  • 10:00 a.m.: Students present web pages,TK TK story pitches, solicit feedback. Shirley Carswell, DJNF Executive Director, visits
  • 12:15 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall
  • 1 p.m.: On the van for D.C.
  • 2:30 p.m.: Tour of African-American History Museum, D.C.
  • 5:30 p.m.: Dinner (Meet at Knight Hall board van)

Sunday June 7, 2026

  • Breakfast available at dining hall.
  • Depart on your own schedule
